flow_name: Quest Data Modeler — AI Features Showcase
cover_narration: AI-first data modeling. In this walkthrough we'll generate a Logical-Physical model for Microsoft Fabric from a single sentence... then iterate on it through the built-in chatbot... and finish by forward-engineering production-ready SQL. All without leaving the canvas.
outro_narration: From plain English to a fully-modeled Microsoft Fabric warehouse — entities, attributes, relationships, and runnable DDL — all driven by the AI right inside the canvas. That's the AI feature set in Quest Data Modeler.

steps.label: Sign in and land on Overview
steps.narration: Sign in to Quest Data Modeler. The Overview page leads with the AI Model Generator.

steps.label: Open the AI Model Generator
steps.narration: Open the AI Model Generator.

steps.label: Open the model-type selector
steps.narration: Pick the model type using the controls under the prompt.

steps.label: Choose Logical/Physical
steps.narration: Choose Logical/Physical so the model carries database metadata for forward engineering.

steps.label: Show LP chip and Microsoft Fabric
steps.narration: Logical/Physical is set... and Microsoft Fabric is the database target.

steps.value: Create a retail data model with three entities for Microsoft Fabric: Customer with id, name, email, phone, created_at; Product with id, name, sku, price, inventory_qty; and Orders with id, customer_id, order_date, total_amount, status. Customer has many Orders.
steps.label: Type the natural language prompt
steps.narration: Now describe the model in plain English... three retail entities and the relationships between them.

steps.label: Submit the AI prompt
steps.narration: Click Create... and the AI gets to work.

steps.label: Wait for AI-generated preview
steps.narration: Within a few seconds the AI proposes a model... a name, three entities with attributes, and pre-wired relationships.

steps.label: Confirm and create the model
steps.narration: Confirm to materialize this as a real Logical-Physical model on the canvas.

steps.label: AI executes the model build plan
steps.narration: The AI walks through its execution plan... creating entities, attributes, and relationships in our Microsoft Fabric model.

steps.label: AI conversation panel ready
steps.narration: Now the canvas is live. On the right, the erwin AI conversation panel is waiting for the next instruction... we can keep iterating on the model in plain English.

steps.label: Focus the chat input
steps.narration: Let's add an OrderLine table for line-item detail.

steps.value: Add a new entity called OrderLine with attributes: id (primary key), order_id, product_id, quantity, unit_price, and line_total.
steps.label: Type 'add OrderLine' instruction
steps.narration: Just type what you want... add an OrderLine entity with id, order_id, product_id, quantity, unit_price, and line_total.

steps.label: Send the OrderLine prompt
steps.narration: Hit Enter. The AI plans the change...

steps.label: AI executes OrderLine creation
steps.narration: and executes it — OrderLine joins the canvas as a real entity with all six attributes.

steps.value: Add foreign key relationships: order_id in OrderLine references Orders.id, and product_id in OrderLine references Product.id.
steps.label: Type relationships instruction
steps.narration: Next... wire up the relationships. Foreign keys from OrderLine into Orders and Product.

steps.label: AI wires the relationships
steps.narration: The AI redraws the canvas with proper foreign-key relationships between OrderLine, Orders, and Product.

steps.value: Add a discount_percent decimal attribute to OrderLine and a loyalty_tier varchar attribute to Customer.
steps.label: Type attribute additions
steps.narration: Need a couple more columns? Same conversation. Add a discount_percent on OrderLine and a loyalty_tier on Customer.

steps.label: AI adds attributes
steps.narration: Both columns are added in seconds — without ever opening a property dialog.

steps.label: Back to Overview for FE
steps.narration: To finish, let's forward-engineer a Logical-Physical model into runnable SQL. Open a Logical-Physical sample.

steps.label: Open the canvas Tools menu
steps.narration: Open the Tools menu in the bottom toolbar.

steps.label: Launch Forward Engineering
steps.narration: Pick Forward Engineering.

steps.label: FE wizard visible
steps.narration: The Forward Engineering wizard walks through the options...

steps.label: Next: Option Selection
steps.narration: what to include...

steps.label: Next: Owner Override
steps.narration: schema owners...

steps.label: Next: Object Filter
steps.narration: object filters...

steps.label: Next: Preview
steps.narration: and straight to the SQL preview.

steps.label: SQL preview rendered
steps.narration: Here's the generated DDL. CREATE TABLE for every entity including the AI-added OrderLine... primary keys, foreign keys, the whole schema... ready to run against Microsoft Fabric Data Warehouse.

steps.label: Close the FE wizard
steps.narration: Close the wizard.
